title: ⭐⭐ Review: Cohesive Devices and Reference Chains

questions.prompt: Choose the CORRECT cohesive device to join these two sentences: "The results were disappointing. ___, the team remained optimistic."
questions.explanation: A contrast is needed: disappointing results vs remaining optimistic. "Nevertheless" (= despite this) signals the contrast perfectly. "Furthermore" and "In addition" add information (wrong relationship). "Therefore" signals a result (wrong direction — the optimism is not caused by the disappointment).

questions.prompt: Find the sentence that INCORRECTLY uses a cohesive device.

options.text: The deadline passed; consequently, the penalty clause was triggered.
Sales increased in Q1. Moreover, they rose further in Q2.
The policy was a success, however it was soon abandoned.
The project ran over budget. As a result, funding was cut.

questions.explanation: "However" is a conjunctive adverb — it cannot join two independent clauses with only a comma (comma splice). It needs either a semicolon before it ("…budget; however, it…") or a new sentence. (a) correctly uses a semicolon before consequently; (b) uses a new sentence before Moreover; (d) uses a new sentence before As a result.

questions.prompt: Choose the BEST cohesive device for this gap: "Many students struggle with time management. ___, they often perform well in individual tasks."
questions.explanation: "Admittedly" signals a concession — acknowledging a true weakness (time management struggles) while pointing out a contrasting strength (good individual performance). It fits the concessive relationship. "Consequently" signals a result (wrong direction); "In conclusion" would only come at the end of an argument; "In addition" adds information without the needed contrast.

questions.prompt:
Read: "A new regulation was introduced last month. ___ regulation requires all firms to disclose their environmental impact."
Which word BEST fills the gap to maintain cohesion?

options.text: Both The and This are correct.

questions.explanation: Both "The" (definite article, signalling the specific regulation already mentioned) and "This" (demonstrative, pointing back to the just-mentioned regulation) correctly create a reference chain. "A" would reintroduce it as if unrelated, breaking the chain — making it seem like a different regulation.

questions.prompt: Identify the function of the cohesive device: "The proposal was well-researched. Furthermore, it was supported by compelling data."
questions.explanation: "Furthermore" signals addition — it introduces an extra point that reinforces or adds to what came before. Both sentences present positive qualities of the proposal; the second adds to the first. There is no contrast, cause-effect relationship, or concession here.

questions.prompt:
Read: "She had intended to apply for the post, but in the end decided not to."
What is omitted after "decided not to" (what does the ellipsis stand for)?
questions.explanation: The full version would be: "…decided not to [apply for the post]". The ellipsis omits the entire infinitive phrase that has already been stated. "Decided not to" is verbal ellipsis — the omitted material (apply for the post) is recoverable from the earlier clause. This is a classic clausal/verbal ellipsis pattern.
